title: History of Copper Fenridge Industries
content:
Copper Fenridge Industries is a precision instruments company founded in 1869 and headquartered in Mossenden. Ulric Corvin served as chief executive during its international expansion phase.

History.

Ulric Corvin negotiated distribution agreements that opened markets in two neighboring republics.

Operations.

Brand spin-offs now cover software diagnostics sold to municipal transport agencies.
